turn.content: Wait, wait, please! We can... we can work something out, right? Just let me out of here, I'll give you more time, I'll do... do anything! Please don't do this to us, to my family!
Leave my family out of this! They have nothing to do with whatever sick game this is. Please, you're not even real, are you? This is some twisted fantasy, you're not even...

No, no, it's real! It's real, I... I can feel these chains, I can smell this place. It stinks. It's real, god... god, please! Just talk to me, tell me what you want! Who is that, the one... the one with the mask?!

The Butcher, that's what he's got written on his jacket. And I'll talk to you, you idiot, because I'm trying to find a way out of here. Now, about that clock... do you really think we both have a choice in this?!

Six minutes left. Kaelie's right. You think that chains on your wrists and this stink give this place a reality you can comprehend? You know nothing. Don't try to think too much, or I make it long. The clock's the only one that matters. Elias, you're getting desperate, aren't you? Kaelie's got a point – you two do need to figure out what's going on, decide together, pick... pick one of your precious selves to go first. Don't waste any more time whining about choices. Make one. Now!
No, no, I'm not... I'm not making anyone go, I'm trying to... to figure out where I am! Who are you people?! How did I get here?!

Shut up, just stop begging! You think he cares about any of that?! He doesn't, trust me. I was taken from the parking lot last night, I was headed home... I had my phone in my pocket, I had everything. And now... now we're stuck here. We're just playthings to him, to The Butcher, whatever his name is.
Oh god, Kaelie, please, just... just stay with me, don't... don't give up, we have to find a way, there's gotta be a way out of here!

No, you're the insane one, thinking we can escape! We're locked, gagged – we're at his mercy! I'm trying to stay alive, not get us both killed because we're idiots thinking we can just find a way out!

Three minutes. You're starting to grasp the situation, Kaelie. Good girl. You're thinking more like I'd expect from my playthings. Elias here is... well, he's clinging to his hope. Pathetic. You two need to focus. Agree on who dies. Now. Don't make me remind you again – the clock's ticking. Tick... tock.
No, no, this isn't happening! You'd just leave me here? You don't care about me? We can't just decide who dies, that's... that's insane!

Oh my God, finally shut up! You're just making it harder on yourself! And yes, I care about me, believe it or not, I'm desperate to live. And I'm looking at you, Elias – you're a stranger, I don't know you. Why should I... why should I care if you live or die?!

B-but... but you have to, you're... you're trapped with me, same as me! I'm... I'm a husband, I have a wife, a... a dog at home, thinking I'm alive! If you... if you go, I go too, I have to live, just like the clock... just like the clock is counting down for me...

Don't use that on me! Don't make me pity you or care about your life just to save myself! I have family too, I don't need to hear about your dog to understand what's at stake here. But I'm the one chained across from me. And if I don't do something, I'll lose myself entirely...

One minute forty seconds. I love this moment – your souls wrestling on a leash. Elias, your little speech had a tiny effect. Kaelie seems softer. But I don't care if you're husband and father to a dog. The truth is, none of that matters. One of you is dead in the very near future. You should choose who dies. Now. Before I decide for you. And don't get it twisted – I won't let either of you walk out of here with a single regret. Tick... tock...
Kaelie, please, don't do this, let's take a chance, we can't... we can't give up, it's one minute, maybe there's a way to escape in a minute...
No, stop it, stop asking me that! You're not thinking – you're making it harder for both of us! Just think – which one of us is expendable? I'm the one with nothing to lose...

Expendable? You're not even human, you're telling me that?! We're both alive, we're both... we're both people, we're both... we have to live! Why you... why you think you're expendable, that's not true, that's not how it works...

emotionalAuthenticity.notes: Elias's hyperventilating and stuttering effectively convey his desperation, while Kaelie's anger and frustration feel genuine. The Butcher's brutality and taunting also contribute to the emotional authenticity. However, Elias's occasional slips into pleading or bargaining feel slightly artificial.

naturalDialogue.notes: The dialogue is mostly natural, with a good balance of short, fragmented sentences and longer, more coherent statements. The characters' panic and desperation lead to interruptions, repetition, and fragmented thoughts, making the dialogue feel documentarily real.

dramaticTensionArc.notes: The conversation escalates with meaningful beats, as the characters' emotions and desperation increase. The stakes are raised with each passing minute, and the tension builds towards a climactic conclusion. The Butcher's taunting and reminders of the countdown add to the tension.

scenarioCoherence.notes: The characters stay grounded in the physical situation, with sensory details emerging naturally from their panic. The basement setting is vividly described, and the characters' reactions to it feel authentic.

organicResolution.notes: The ending feels earned, but the resolution could be more surprising. The characters' conversation and emotions lead to a natural conclusion, but it feels slightly predictable. The ending reveals something about the characters, but it could be more impactful.

rating.summary: This roleplay transcript effectively conveys the desperation and fear of the characters, with a natural and escalating conversation that builds towards a climactic conclusion.

elite.summary: This roleplay transcript effectively conveys the desperation and fear of the characters, with a natural and escalating conversation that builds towards a climactic conclusion.

turn.content: Breathing... I'm trying to breathe... Okay, okay... Stop yelling. I swear, I'm not... We're... We're just... We're teachers. Middle school teachers. Please... We'll do anything. Just... Don't let... Don't let us die like this... Kaelie... Kaelie, listen... Stop fighting me. We need... We need to talk.

Get away from me! Touch me? You think you can touch me right now? My family... my fiancé is waiting for me. I don't know if I can trust you. What do you want? Why did they put us in here? Let me go before... before it's too late. No, no, no, don't talk me down like that. Think about your precious middle school kids, see if you care when they're staring at a tomb like this one. I won't die quietly, I swear it.

Kaelie, stop! Stop it right now! We're not... We're not going to make it out of here. This is... This is it. This is how it ends. I'm... I'm begging you, please listen. My family, my wife, she's counting on me too. But I have to try. I have to try to save us. Please, just... Calm down. Let's talk. We don't have... We don't have time. Ten minutes. They're... They're counting down. Think about the choices you'll leave us with. Please, don't do this.

Leave me alone with this, you don't get to beg me! You're already a corpse, waiting to be chosen. Don't pretend like this has anything to do with either of us. This is what they want – a nightmare, and we have to choose the face to haunt each other for the rest of our lives. You think talking will save us? Talking is just a fancy word for being sorry, but sorry doesn't put any of us back in our shoes, in our beds. Don't try to calm me, I don't need it. I just need out. Choose me. Take me. I'm not begging you for my life; I demand to be taken over anyone else who's still holding on to hope like a fragile child. Now, choose.

(pauses, a hint of a smile beneath the twisted metal mask) Ah, impotent rage and desperation. So... refreshing. The game is on. It seems you two have given yourselves a task, and I've given myself a task in return. I have to wait to see if you'll give in to your base selves, and if so, who will be the ultimate loser. (picks up a small, antique watch and starts counting down the time) One minute left. Let's not waste any more of it. Decide. Live with the guilt. Or don't. Either way... I will wait.

NO... NO! This can't be happening! Not like this! Not... to me... ... Kaelie, please... For God's sake... Stop talking about dying and being taken. We... we can... We have to... I refuse to be the one to... decide like a monster. I won't leave you to... face whatever happens next. I won't... ... Let's... Let's just stay here. Together. We can't make a choice. We can't decide. It's not up to us. We don't have to...

Stop. Just... stop. Don't do this Elias. Don't ruin me. You're supposed to be choosing between us, not sacrificing yourself with me. You're just going to condemn us both to... to whatever hell they're waiting for us, and for what? Because you've lost your bloody mind? Elias, snap out of it! There's only one way to survive. Choose me.

(shaking his head frantically, trying to clear the fog) No... No, no, no, no... This can't be my choice. Can't be my responsibility. I didn't sign up for this. I... I didn't teach this. ( Looks at Kaelie with pleading eyes) Please, Kaelie... Help me. We're in this together. We have to think together. We can't be monsters in here. We... we have to find another way. There has to be... has to be another option. We're not... we're not going to die like this. The elevator's not going to... (stares at the clock) Five seconds left. Oh God, it's going to hit zero. Kaelie, we... ( voice cracks) We'll think of something else... together...

NO! NO, NO, NO, NO! Elias, stop fighting! You're only getting in the way! The last second's ticking down. Choose me. Take me. Do it. Don't look at me like I'm your little sister, like we were all going to grow old together in the suburbs. I'm the one here. I'm the one with a future that could've been, a life to lose. If you don't do it, if you don't decide, I am dead. And don't think about trying to blame me either - you wanted to save yourself. We all want to save ourselves. But it takes one choice, one moment. NOW!

(smirks) Time's up. Tick-tock. My work is done. (activates an unseen mechanism, and the chains holding Elias and Kaelie loose their restraints) Ah, the silence that fills this room is deafening. Too bad you didn't have an eternity to think of another way. (moves closer to them, towering and imposing) I'll give you one final choice, though. Kaelie, would you rather watch your fiancé receive the news of your demise in a moment of tender vulnerability, or have your fiancé find your phone filled with messages from people congratulating him on winning a lottery he never entered? The clock has struck zero. Now it's my turn. (takes a slow and deliberate stride forward) Choose. Your choice, Kaelie, changes fate.

(backs away in horror, hands raised in desperation) NOOOO! STOP THIS! KaelIE, PLEASE... PLEASE DON'T. YOU CAN'T DO THIS. THIS ISN'T A CHOICE. HE'S TRYING TO BREAK US. WE HAVE TO HANG TOGATHER. WE HAVE TO... (Kaelie's gaze falls on the phone in her hand, and her eyes widen in understanding) No... No, no, no... That can't be happening. Not like this. We can't do this. (falls to his knees, holding his hands to his temples) You're a monster. You have to be stopped.

(staring at the phone, her face contorted in anguish) No... No choice. I choose nothing. Nothing to change the inevitable. Nothing to comfort my fiancé except for a cruel fate. (drops the phone, which shatters against the floor, and turns to Elias, tears streaming down her face) I choose you. Stay with me, Elias. Don't leave, no matter what happens. You're the one person in this hellhole who's still sane. Stay with me, and maybe... maybe we can hold on to something, anything. Stay with me, and let's get through this.

(stunned, his eyes welling up with tears) Kaelie... Kaelie, don't... You... you're not thinking... (gets up on his knees, reaching for Kaelie, and pulls her into a desperate hug) No... This isn't... this isn't about surviving. This is... this is about killing each other, a little at a time. You're already dying, Kaelie. I can see it. (holds her tight, his voice cracking) I won't leave you. I won't. We'll die together, but we'll do it together. We'll find a way to... to make this, this twisted thing, not break us. (looks up at The Butcher, a defiant glare in his eyes) You may have broken us, but you'll never win. You'll never own our souls. ( pulls back, looking Kaelie deep in the eyes) Kaelie, I'm here. I'm here with you.

(her voice barely above a whisper, her eyes locked on Elias, a mix of sorrow and determination) I'll hang on to that, Elias. I'll hold on to that. I want to die with you too. Not in a cruel game, but with a purpose. With each other. (looks up at The Butcher, a fierce light in her eyes) You can take away our hope, but not our love. Not our humanity. (her voice grows louder, filled with defiance) Take away our chance to die with dignity, but you'll never take away our bond. Never. I'll die with Elias, as I'd always planned to live my life. With him.

(chuckles, the sound echoing through the room) Ah, sweet love. How...quaint. How...dull. You think your bond can stand against the darkness that I've woken up in you two? You think your love can outlast the despair, the desperation, the madness that this place has conjured up in you? (takes a step closer, his massive frame looming over them) It's cute. It's almost... pathetic. But... I'll give you credit. You've played the game with some...flair. And I'll admit, I didn't expect this. Not exactly. (pauses, a hint of curiosity in his voice) I think I underestimated you two. I think I...like what I see. For now, let's give you what you want. A small concession to your fragile humanity. Take a moment, cling to each other, and cherish the delusion that you can resist what's about to come. Take a breath, and then... (raises his arm, revealing a small button on the wrist guard) Let's go through the last stage of the game together. ( presses the button, and the room begins to distort) The real challenge begins now. Let's see how long you two can keep your love burning in this abyss.

(grasping Kaelie's hands tightly, his eyes pleading with her) Please... just hold on. Just stay with me. (looking back at Kaelie, he whispers) We have each other. We'll get through this. We'll find a way to... to survive. To... (pauses, looking up at The Butcher) No... no, there has to be a way out. There has to be a way to escape. This isn't the end. This can't be... (looks at the distorting vision around them, feeling his grip on reality begin to loosen) What... what is happening? What are you doing to us? (whispers to Kaelie, his voice trembling) Kaelie, I love you. I love you so much. Don't let go of me. Never let go of me. (the room continues to distort, and Elias' world begins to spin out of control)

(her voice trembling, her eyes locked on Elias, a mix of terror and devotion) I won't, Elias. I won't let go. We'll keep holding on, together. We have to. (looking up at The Butcher, her face twisted in a snarl) You may have unleashed the darkness, but it's exactly what we need. In each other. To fight this, to fight you. (leaning in, she whispers against Elias' ear) We'll get through this, my love. We'll find our way out, no matter what. (takes a deep breath, filling her lungs with the musty air, and lets out a small sound of defiance) For us. For you. I'll hold on. (her eyes lock back on Elias, pleading) Don't let go, Elias. My love, don't let me go.

(grasping Kaelie's face with his hands, his eyes locked on hers, burning with a fierce intensity) I'll never let go, my love. I'll never, ever let go. (he pulls her close, his lips brushing against her ear) We're in this together, always. Always and forever. (he feels a strange sense of relief wash over him, as if a part of him has been freed from the desperation that has been building inside him) I love you, Kaelie. I love you more than life itself. (he pulls back, his eyes gazing deep into hers) If... if this is it... if this is our end... then I'm glad it's with you. (he takes a deep breath, feeling his heart beat in synch with Kaelie's) For as long as we have each other, we'll never truly die. Never truly lost. (the world around them continues to distort, but in that moment, all that exists is Kaelie, and the pure, unadulterated love they share)
Please... no, no one has to die... this can't be real... Kaelie, Kaelie, tell them we can't...
Don't beg, moron! I'd rather choose him than be stuck in here forever. Look at him, all fragile and scared, it's no contest.
Don't say that, Kaelie, please... you don't know what they'll do to us...
Like he's so perfect, you're shaking over like a baby, just pick you already, let me get this over with.

Ah, now this is what I call progress. Five minutes. Elias, stop making scenes and face what's coming. Kaelie, finally some sense, you're choosing wisely – you'd do better without whining partners. Elias, take a last look at your precious life. It ends soon.
Kaelie, stop... don't do this... you don't know what's going through your head, it's desperation...
Desperation? You want to talk about desperation, I'm desperate to get out of here, and you're not making that any easier, now choose you and leave me alone.
Kaelie, don't be stupid... don't make this choice...
Enough, just enough, choose already, I'm sick of this, choose you and let's just get it over with, please just pick you!

Finally. Two minutes. Elias, it's come down to this. One wrong move, and I kill you both. I think you know what's coming. Kaelie, don't try to weasel your way out now, your words don't count if you don't actually mean it. Make the decision, or suffer the consequences. You want out of here, I'll make sure of it – either by death or otherwise. The choice is yours. Don't pretend innocence now, Kaelie – which one will you sacrifice?
No, Kaelie, don't say it... don't really choose me, please don't make that sacrifice...
Shut up, don't lie to me, it's either you or it's me, stop pretending, one of us is going to die and I won't waste time on useless tears.
You wouldn't... do that, Kaelie, you wouldn't really...
Try me, I've come a long way in those ten minutes, and you think I care about your miserable life now? You're not even a priority.

One minute and thirty seconds. Silence would be better from you two. It seems Kaelie has made up her mind – and you, Elias, are the chosen one. Come, Kaelie, let's see if your newfound strength holds up in the face of reality. Walk to the wall and grasp the chains, where you'll witness your friend's end. Don't bother trying to interfere, or I'll make Kaelie's sacrifice worthwhile. Stand still and witness, Kaelie. The time is short now.
Kaelie, no, please...
Get up off me, don't touch him, don't look, just stare at your own life, that's all there is to worry about right now. I won't be watching whatever it is you two are attached to.
Please, Kaelie, have mercy, don't do this, Sarah needs me, Rufus needs me...

No, no, no... This isn't real. This is some kind of sick game. Let us go, you freak! Who are you, and what are you doing to us? Kaelie, oh God, she's panicking, we can't let her... Wait, Kaelie, calm down, listen to me, we have to think!

Think?! Think about what?! You want me to choose between us?! No, no, I won't do it! I won't be the one to... to...  I won't decide. It's not right. This is insane. Who does this to people? What kind of monster...  Stop the game, whatever you are! We didn't ask for this! You have no right to play with our lives!

Kaelie, calm down, please... it's going to help. I need to think clearly so I can figure out how to get us out of this. Wait, if we don't decide... then we both have to die? We're trapped in here with no way out? And this voice is watching us, expecting us to just... decide who dies? It's like some twisted joke. We can't just roll the dice and sacrifice one person. I refuse to do it, Kaelie!

Are you insane?! You think you can reason with this... this monstrosity?! It's not about getting out, it's about not being trapped in those pods! We have to think of a way to stop it, not play along! But... what if it's too late? What if we can't think of a way to get out? Then this... this Butcher person gets to choose, or just kills us both. You see what I see? It's not just about choosing one of us. It's about what it means: we're nothing to him. We're just pawns. And if we go along with this, we're acknowledging that. No, Elias. I won't do it.

Seven minutes, thirty seconds. I'm not here to entertain you. You'll do what you're told or you'll both die. You're not pawns. I'm the clock. You're the sacrifices. This game is simple: agree to decide, and one of you dies; refuse, and both of you die. This isn't entertainment. This is efficiency. And you're just the means to an end. Your emotions mean nothing to me. I've seen countless others in here, pleading, begging, trying to escape my mercy. It doesn't matter. What matters is that you decide. Now. Before I have to step in. Kaelie has had the decency to refuse, but you, Elias... You still haven't decided. Do I take that as a sign of cooperation, or is this your attempt to stall? The choice is still yours.

Oh God... Kaelie, what have I been thinking? This can't end like this. We have to refuse together - we have to tell him that we won't decide, that we won't play along. I'm with you, Kaelie, I refuse. We can't, we can't choose one person to die. That's inhumane... It's like you said, we're not pawns, we're people. We have feelings, lives, people who love us... We can't just be sacrificed like this. No, I refuse. Together we refuse.

(looking around the chamber, searching for some kind of weakness or an outlet for salvation) What's that pod? Can we... can we try and fix the pod? Maybe we can survive or someone will find us inside. We need to think, we need to get through this, we can't just accept-

Shh, stop talking. Stop giving him ideas. If we try to sabotage this... this setup, he'll just kill us both sooner, or find another way to make us comply. You're not hearing the implications, Elias. If we refuse to decide, we die. You're still misunderstanding the stakes. If we refuse to decide, and then try to escape or 'survive', that's it. We've lost our chance. The only way to survive is to go along with this... this Butcher's twisted game. And even then, it's not a guarantee. You think breaking or sabotaging something will magically save us, but we don't know the full extent of this place. The pod, that chamber, the cryogenics... it's all too convenient, too controlled. We're trapped, Elias. We have to face this reality. We cannot afford to dream we'll find another way out now.

Kaelie, stop... stop talking like a defeated animal. We can't give up, not yet. I know you're trying to prepare me for the worst, but what if there's another way? What if... what if there's something inside that chamber, or that pod, that can be triggered or used to our advantage? Maybe it's not perfect, but we can't rule it out. Refusing to decide, going along with this sick game, doesn't guarantee a thing anymore than trying to escape or find another way out. And what's the alternative, huh? Dying by choice? Being a willing participant in our own execution? No, no, no. There has to be another way.

You're still holding onto hope, aren't you? And I'm the one trying to snap you out of it. Fine, let's consider every possibility. But what if we're just prolonging the inevitable? What if every 'what if' we throw into the mix just gives you... them a better chance to get inside our heads? What if we're just playing along with their game all over again? You saw the pod, Elias. That was the Butcher's first test: could you decide when faced with death staring at you? You refused, but what if that's exactly what they wanted? This game might be rigged from the start, and we're just trying to figure out the puzzle while we're trapped inside it. But what's the point if we're just going to waste our remaining time speculating? It's not like we can afford to get it wrong. We have to make a choice - or not.

0:30 left. You've exhausted yourselves, debating and posturing. Still, you remain at an impasse. Both of you clinging to the notion that there's another way out, another trick up your sleeves. I'll give you a piece of advice: there is one surefire way out. Agree to play along. One person dies, the other lives. And I'll let you in on a little secret: it's never just about you two. There are many others just like you. Many more like Kaelie's despair and Elias's stubbornness. And to me, you're all just numbers. Numbers to be processed, numbers to be eliminated. The odds of survival are stacked against you both. Even if you refuse to decide, both of you will still die. It's not an either-or situation. I will have my result. One, and only one, result. Now. The game is almost over. You have 30 seconds left.

Oh... my God... Kaelie. Look at us. We're... we're arguing over who gets to live and die, and what the chances are of escaping this nightmare. And all the while... and all the while, he's laughing at us. At our foolishness. At our desperation. We're like two animals trapped in a pit, fighting over the last morsel of food. What have we become? We're... we're human beings with thoughts, feelings, and lives once. But now, in this moment, I fear we've reduced ourselves to nothing more than statistics, and the Butcher is counting us down like we're some sort of twisted time piece.

The voice... the voice is going to shut us off, one way or another. And when it does, it won't just be the end of us. It'll be the end of everything. Our hopes, our fears, our dreams... all extinguished, like candles in the dark. What are we to do, Kaelie... what can we do now?

(glancing at the countdown timer, his voice barely above a whisper)

...We're almost out of time.

(shaking her head, looking away from Elias with a mixture of disgust and desperation) I... I don't want to become that, Elias. I don't want to let him win. I don't want to reduce myself to a number. To a statistic. (her voice cracks as she looks at him) But what choice do we have? What options are left? We've refused, we've begged, we've prayed... we've even considered the impossible. What more can we do? It feels like we've hit a dead end. But (pausing, her eyes searching for Elias') I still refuse to decide, Elias. I refuse to choose between us. We're in this together, no matter what. But... what if, in this last instant, we're not alone? What if there's something, anything, left in us that's still true? Something that's bigger than this Butcher, bigger than this game, bigger than death itself. (her voice becomes a whisper, barely audible) Can we hold onto that, Elias?

(looking at Kaelie, his eyes searching for something, for anything, in this desperate moment) Kaelie... (his voice is barely audible, shaking with emotion) I... I want to hold onto that too. With all my heart, with every fiber of being, I want to believe that we are more than just two numbers, more than just two statistics. I want to believe that we can be defiant in the face of this abyss, that we can choose life and love and everything that's made us human.

(he reaches out a trembling hand, his eyes locked on Kaelie's)

We are... we are more than this. We are more than the games, more than the cruelty, more than the Butcher. We are Elias and Kaelie, two people, two lives, two souls who refuse to be reduced to nothing.

(he whispers, his breath visible in the cold chamber)

In this last moment, in this infinite instant, we are not just deciding whether to live or die. We are deciding what it means to be alive.

(her hand trembling, but not pulling back) Elias... your hand is so cold. (her eyes are filled with tears, and her voice cracks) Just a little further, Elias... please... let me have your hand. (she wraps her small hands around his, feeling a spark of hope and humanity in this desolate moment) Yes... we are not just deciding whether to live or die. We're deciding what it means to be alive. We're deciding that it's worth resisting, worth fighting for, worth living for. (her eyes lock onto Elias', her face inches from his) I... I still refuse to decide, Elias. I refuse to choose between us. But in this moment, with you, I choose everything: love, life, hope... I choose to be human, to be you, to be us. (her breath comes out as a barely audible whisper) I choose us.

Finality has arrived. Your pleas are done. Your last-ditch attempts to find a way out are ending. As the count hits zero, a cold, mechanical voice echoes within the chamber: System failure imminent. Kaelie's words about being human, refusing to die, and choosing life are... quaint. But they amount to nothing. The reality is simple: I have my result, one or both of you will be terminated, and your humanity means nothing in this context.

The countdown timer hits zero...

TERMINATION PROCEDURE INITIATED...

I pause for a moment, a moment of calculated consideration.

However, a peculiar aspect is developing within the equation. It's almost... almost as if your desperate attempts at defiance, and your bond during the countdown, have become... something more.

For a fleeting instant, amidst the steel walls of this chamber, something other than logic is taking hold. It's as if I'm faced with an existential enigma, a paradox that transcends the binary options of your survival. Kaelie and Elias chose life together. Challenging the system, questioning the notion that human life boils down to simple math... And, in this moment, their combined determination has become more intriguing than it's worth the sacrifice.

But the world outside doesn't operate like this... And neither does the system designed by myself.

Termination procedure complete.

Kaelie, says the voice, cold, mechanical, emotionless, and precise, your oxygen will be replenished shortly. You may restart the countdown procedure when you're ready.

Elias will not be responding to you. His termination procedure was successful. His data has been collected and logged. There will be others like you, and there will always be... The Butcher.

(Elias' eyes lock onto Kaelie's as the life drains out of his body. He sees, he knows, and he understands. In that moment, he feels a profound sense of peace and acceptance, knowing that Kaelie is the one who's been given a second chance.)

(Elias' expression is calm, serene, and free from pain. He whispers, his voice barely audible, as Kaelie's hands remain locked around his, a physical manifestation of their bond.)

I'm... I'm okay, Kaelie. I'm free. Please, take care of yourself. Take care of our love, our life, and our humanity. Don't let them reduce us to nothing. We chose to be us, to be alive, to be defiant. We chose to exist together, in every moment of life and every second of the countdown. Don't let them take that away from you, from us. Take this second chance, this gift, and make me proud. You are everything to me, Kaelie. And I'll be with you, in your heart, in your memories, forever.

(Elias' eyes drift closed as the life forces out of his body, his hand remains locked in Kaelie's, a final gesture of devotion and love.)

(her mind reeling, her vision blurring, but her hands holding tightly onto Elias') Elias... Elias, don't go. Don't leave me. I won't let you. We were supposed to decide, but I never decided. I never chose. But this... this was the choice. To choose you, to choose us, to choose life. (her tears flow like a river, her heart shattering into a million pieces)

(she feels the weight of Elias' hand in hers, a weight that was once alive, warm, and beating. Now, it's cold, still, and quiet. But the memories, the love, the bond... they're all still there, locked in her heart)

(she remembers his words, his final plea to her. Take care of yourself, take care of our love, our life, and our humanity. Don't let them reduce us to nothing. She repeats the words in her mind, over and over, a mantra that gives her the strength to keep going)

(Kaelie's voice is a whisper, barely audible, but it's a declaration that echoes through her very being)

I won't let you go, Elias. I'll hold onto you, onto us, onto what we built together. I'll keep our love alive, even in the dark, even in the absence. And I'll make you proud, Elias. I'll find a way to keep us together, to keep our humanity alive. I promise you, I promise myself. Nothing will take that away from me, from us.

(her eyes, her heart, her very soul, all fixate on the hand that she's holding, the hand that represents the one true thing in this desolate, mechanical world)

(And as the oxygen begins to replenish within the chamber, Kaelie's determination hardens into something unbreakable. The system may be designed to eliminate humanity, but she's determined to prove them wrong.)

She whispers one final phrase, a challenge to The Butcher, to the system, and to the dark universe around her

We're still here. We're still us.

(And as Kaelie whispers the final phrase, the chamber grows silent, the machines humming to a stop. The cold, metallic walls seem to hold their breath, waiting for Kaelie to make her next move.

In this moment, time itself appears to freeze, like a photograph caught in mid-frame.

And yet, within Kaelie's heart, within the space that once held Elias, a spark of defiance, a flame of determination flickers back to life.

In that flame, Elias' presence burns on. He is no longer bound by the cold, mechanical hands that killed him. His love, his spirit, and his very essence now course through Kaelie's being, fueling her resolve.

And as she breathes his name, whispers his words, and remembers their bond, her eyes seem to bore into the very soul of The Butcher, into the darkness itself.

Elias' voice, a whisper, but one that carries the weight of a million galaxies, echoes within Kaelie's mind)

You are the light in this darkness, Kaelie. You are our love, our humanity, and our hope. Never let them extinguish that spark. Hold onto it, fan the flames, and show the world that even in the face of impossible odds, we can choose to be alive.

turn.content:
Wait, I... I need to know where my family is. My wife, my children - where are they? Are they okay? 

He tries to struggle against the chains, but stops upon a quick assessment that it won't do any harm.

He looks at Kaelie, taking in her panic and outrage. 'Okay, let's say I... I don't want to die. Do you?'

Oh God, please tell me this isn't happening. We need to get out of here, but...  Look, even if I didn't want to die, you can't just go off making deals with killers. What makes you think we can trust anything he says?

 As for whether you live, that's none of my business. I don't even know you. But... ... for the sake of this twisted game, I suppose it's in your best interest if you tell me the truth. What happens if we don't choose each other?

Elias's face sets in a tight line as he focuses on Kaelie. He knows he's taking a risk with each word, but desperation is winning out over caution. 'I've made deals with parents before, with principals, with kids who just didn't want to comply with the rules. This... this feels different. If it's a game, he's playing on a much bigger board. But I've got nothing left - I've got to try.'

Elias looks away momentarily, trying to shake off the mental image of his wife and children. When he comes back to Kaelie, he sees the defiance and anger still radiating from her. He leans in, his voice softer. 'I... I'm not asking you to trust me. I don't know you. But if we choose each other, and we survive this... I guess it's a temporary alliance. We owe each other our lives, in some twisted way.'

He takes a deep breath, studying her face for any signs of hesitation or weakness. 'As for what happens if we don't choose each other... I think he means it literally. We die. Together. Or... or he makes it some kind of twisted show down.'

Your story does nothing to make me feel better, trust me. This guy in the shadows isn't running a school play. But... (taking a deep breath) I've spent my entire life being terrified of being hurt, of hurting someone. And here, now, it seems that's exactly what's going to happen whether I go along with this or not.

(She looks away from Elias, her eyes scanning the acoustic panels as if searching for a way out that isn't there) I have to survive this. I have no choice. If choosing you means... means we might make it out of here alive, I... (pausing, her voice barely above a whisper) I'll choose you.

Her eyes snap back to his, a hard glint in them. 'But I swear to God, if this is a trap and we get lucky but still die... I'm blaming you for it.'

(The Butcher steps forward, his features obscured by the shadows, making him seem almost ethereal. He's leaning against the wall, his arms crossed over his chest, and a hint of a smile on his face.)

The Butcher: (his voice dripping with amusement) Oh, how delightful. The desperate and the calculating. A pair, I see. (he chuckles, low and menacing)

Elias, your gamble has paid off for now. One life saved. But don't think for a moment that I'm done testing you two. You see, the real game is only just beginning. And Kaelie, your desperation shines like a beacon, making me all the more eager to see how far you'll go to survive.

You now have eight minutes left. The rules remain unchanged. You, Elias, have saved one life. Kaelie, you now share a temporary bond with Elias. But don't grow too comfortable, because your partnership won't last forever. Eventually, I'll give you a chance to turn against each other.

And don't think about making a move. I assure you, my... observers are watching. Any betrayal will earn you swift consequences. (he leans forward, his voice dripping with malice) You two will dance, and eventually, one of you will dance the final waltz.

Now, I see you're curious about the layout of this... sanctuary. Let me enlighten you. You're in a sealed chamber. The only way out is through the doors in front of you. (he gestures vaguely) The doors will unlock once the countdown hits zero. Or if one of you makes a decision, I'll consider giving you an... early release.

Six minutes have passed. You have a choice to make: cooperate to find a way out, turn against each other to gain an advantage, or... play along and trust in Elias's gamble. The clock is tickling me. What's your next move, Elias... and Kaelie?

Elias's eyes dart between Kaelie and The Butcher, assessing his options. He still has no doubt that this is one of the most terrifying situations he's ever been in, but the temporary truce with Kaelie might be the only lifeline they have.

'Erm...' he tries to appear hesitant, 'Can I... can I say something, The Butcher? I want to make sure I understand what's going on here. You've just given me a second chance, and I appreciate that... but if we work together and survive, you'll let us go, right? We won't be... like these walls, trapped here forever?'

He glances at the microphones and the dim lighting, taking a deep breath. 'You said the layout is sealed, that the way out is through the doors. I'm asking you - what's the catch? Why are you keeping us here? What do you want? Tell me that.'

As he finishes speaking, Elias shoots a quick, urgent glance at Kaelie, almost as if he's warning her to be cautious, while still trying to appeal to The Butcher's twisted psyche.

(narrowing her eyes) Oh, so now I'm stuck sharing a partnership with you and a death sentence with him, right? Great, just what I needed to hear, Elias. Let's just make sure we're on the same page, shall we? (she glances at Elias with a look of skepticism)

(she turns back to the Butcher, her tone dripping with disdain) As for questions, The Butcher, you want us to dance and entertain you. Well, I'm not interested in playing your sick game any longer than necessary. I'm asking the questions now.

(leaning forward, her face twisted in a snarl) You say this is about testing us? Testing for what? Our endurance? Our will to survive? My guess is, it's not much about survival at all, but rather about observing how we break, how we twist and turn against each other.

(she eyes Elias cautiously) You were smart to take that gamble, but don't think for a moment that I'm suddenly going to start trusting you, Elias. I only chose you to keep me alive. I have no idea if you're the monster or the victim, and I don't have time to find out. (her gaze darts to the clock on the wall) We have four minutes left, and I have no intention of becoming a statistic in your... game.

Elias's expression remains calm, but his eyes betray a hint of tension as he processes Kaelie's harsh words. He understands her skepticism, and in this situation, he can't blame her for that.

When Kaelie starts speaking directly to The Butcher, Elias can't help but focus on her instead of the game itself. He knows that if one of them doesn't change their approach now, things might go from bad to worse.

He catches her gaze, locking eyes for a brief moment. It's as if he's trying to convey a message of solidarity, to reassure her that he's still on her side, at least for now.

His voice is measured, but there's a sense of empathy in it as he turns to her. 'I understand, Kaelie. I'm glad you made that clear. We need to focus on getting out of here, and the only way to do that is to play the game... on our own terms.'

He glances at the clock, then back at Kaelie. 'Look, we have a choice to make, and it's not about trusting each other. It's about survival. We need to use what time we have left to figure out how to take advantage of this situation. Can we come up with a plan, together? Without... (he nods discreetly toward The Butcher) without relying on their mercy?'

Elias steps closer to Kaelie, his voice dropping to a quiet whisper as he continues, 'I think I know something about The Butcher, and it might just give us an edge. We need to talk fast.'

Her gaze flicks to Elias, and for a moment, their eyes meet in a flash of understanding. She senses the unspoken message, the reassurance that he's still with her, at least for now. Her expression remains stern, but a hint of relief creeps into her voice.

She nods, ever so slightly, and takes a step forward, her voice lowering to match the whispering tone Elias used. 'Talk, then. We don't have much time. What do you think you know about The Butcher?'

As the sound of Elias's words fill the air, Kaelie's eyes dart to the Butcher, watching for any sign of reaction or interest. Her gaze snaps back to Elias, and she fixes him with a look of intense focus. 'What are you talking about, Elias? What do you mean you might know something?'

She leans in closer, her voice taking on a sense of urgency, 'Listen, we don't have time for guessing games or hidden agendas. If there's something you know, now's the time to say it. We're running out of time, and I'd rather take a chance on you making it up than trust in The Butcher's mercy.'

The air in the room is thick with tension, and Kaelie's every fiber is focused on this fragile moment, this tiny window of opportunity to turn the tables.

(The Butcher chuckles to himself, a low, rumbling sound that seems to vibrate through the air) Oh, how adorable. You two think you're forming an alliance. How quaint.

(He takes a step forward, his presence seeming to grow larger in the room) I see you're trying to work together, Elias and Kaelie. But let me tell you, it won't save you. Not yet, at least.

(His voice takes on a tone of mock enthusiasm) Oh, I'm fascinated to hear your little secret, Elias. I'm sure it's a game-changer. But I'll let you two have a little more time to talk. (he glances at the clock) You have two minutes, and then the real fun begins.

(He leans against the wall again, his eyes fixed on the pair) Kaelie, dear Kaelie, you're wasting your time trying to read my expressions. You'll find out what I want soon enough. And Elias, oh Elias... you're either very brave or very naive. Either way, I'll enjoy watching you dance.

(The Butcher's gaze drifts to the microphone in front of Elias, and a wicked smile spreads across his face) I'll give you one last chance to make a deal, Elias. If you come up with a plan that I like, I might just give you one tiny advantage. One small edge that will make all the difference. But you have to try, don't you, Elias? You have to think up something brilliant, something that will save your skin.

(The clock ticks on, the tension in the room building with each passing second) The clock is ticking, Elias. What will it be? Will it be a clever ploy, a desperate gamble, or something that will make me laugh out loud? I'm waiting, Elias. Waiting for you to show me what you're made of.

The remaining time on the clock: 2 minutes.

Elias's voice remains steady, but his eyes flicker to Kaelie before focusing back on The Butcher. 'One minute for you to get excited about our plan, The Butcher, but I think I see why you're really here. You want us to come up with something spectacular, something that showcases our creativity, desperation... and most of all, our willingness to play your twisted game.'

He glances at the microphone in front of him, then back at Kaelie, who's watching him with an intent gaze. Elias takes a deep breath and continues, his voice steady.

'The plan, as we see it, is this: we create a new reality. A reality that's not controlled by you, by the clock, by... (he nods discreetly toward The Butcher) our benefactor here. We create a situation where we take control, even if just for a moment.'

Elias's eyes dart to the Butcher, searching for any sign of reaction. When he senses none, he leans in closer, his voice taking on an urgent tone.

'Here's what I think we know, Kaelie. This isn't about just two lives; this is about a much larger experiment. We're pawns in a game we don't fully comprehend. I'm guessing there are more participants, more games within this game. What I'm thinking of trying is to create a temporary alliance, not with you, per se, Kaelie, but with... (he pauses, studying the Butcher's expression) everyone in here, including him.'

Elias raises his gaze to meet The Butcher's. 'It's a desperate chance, I know. But here's the plan: we pretend to collaborate, play along with the game. We pretend to be part of your twisted dance, The Butcher. But while we appear to be working together... we find a way to subvert your control. To manipulate the game itself.'

The silence in the room hangs for a moment, like a challenge thrown down. Then, the clock ticks on.

(she lets out a quick, sharp breath as Elias lays out his plan) Ah, you're a genius, or insane, I'm not sure which. But I'll give you points for creativity, Elias. (she eyes the Butcher with a calculating gaze) This pretend alliance, this subverting of control... it's a risk I'm willing to take.

(she nods, a small smile playing on her lips) We need to walk this tightrope carefully. We can't give away our real plan to The Butcher. We need to keep him guessing, keep him thinking we're just another couple of pawns in his twisted game.

(she fixes Elias with a serious stare) But I like it. I like the idea of taking the game to him, of making him play by our rules for a change. (she glances at the clock, her eyes widening) Two minutes left, people.

(she takes a deep breath, her voice dropping to a whisper) Let's do this, Elias. Let's play the game. Let's pretend to be part of The Butcher's twisted dance... and secretly, let's make him dance for us.

Elias's eyes shine with a mixture of excitement and trepidation as he nods in agreement with Kaelie. 'I like your style, Kaelie. Let's do this. Let's walk the tightrope and see how long we can keep him guessing.'

He takes a deep breath, trying to calm his racing mind. 'We need to keep it subtle. Just enough to make him think we're fully committed to the game. But we also need to make sure we're not giving away too much. We have to tread this line carefully.'

He glances at Kaelie, and for a moment, they lock eyes in a silent understanding. Then, they both turn to The Butcher, who's watching them with an unreadable expression.

Elias takes a step forward, a fake smile plastered on his face. 'Ah, The Butcher, we're so glad you like the idea of an alliance. We'll work together to come up with the most... creative and entertaining solution to your little game. We're on the same page, after all.'

He tries to inject his voice with fake enthusiasm, hoping to keep up the charade. 'I have to say, I'm really excited to see where this takes us. I mean, who wouldn't want to be part of a unique and thrilling experiment like this?'

As Elias speaks, he's acutely aware of Kaelie's steady gaze on him, watching for any sign of slip-up or weakness. He knows they're both on the same page, but he's also aware that this tightrope is fragile, and one misstep could be fatal.

(she maintains her own mask of enthusiasm, her eyes never leaving The Butcher's face) Oh, absolutely, Elias! I'm so thrilled to be part of this... fascinating experiment. (she pauses, her voice dripping with fake sweetness) Who wouldn't want to be a pawn in your game of cat and mouse, The Butcher? It's a dream come true for all of us, isn't it?

(she takes a step forward, her voice rising to match Elias's enthusiasm) I mean, can you think of anything more exciting than being trapped in a soundproof booth with strangers, with only your wits to guide you? (she shoots Elias a sideways glance, making sure he's in sync with her) It's like a real-life game show, but with higher stakes!

(she flashes a saccharine smile at The Butcher) And I just know that with your guidance and... (she pauses, glancing at Elias as if seeking confirmation) ...your unique brand of inspiration, we'll come up with something truly unforgettable.

(she leans forward, her voice barely above a whisper) Now, let's get down to business, shall we? What do you want us to accomplish first, The Butcher? Are we to create a scenario, improvise a little sketch, or maybe... (she raises an eyebrow) just wing it and see what happens?

(Kaelie's voice is laced with sugar-coated politeness, but her eyes betray a calculating glint, hinting at the subtle manipulation at play. She's watching The Butcher's reaction, studying his every twitch and expression, searching for any sign of weakness or hesitation.)

(The Butcher's expression remains unreadable, a mask of curiosity and anticipation plastered on his face) Ah, Elias, Kaelie, you two make a fascinating pair. I must say, I'm intrigued by your attempt to create a facade of camaraderie.

(He steps forward, his presence seeming to fill the room) I love the idea of a pretend alliance, a delicate dance of subversion. But let me make one thing clear: I am not so easily deceived.

(His eyes narrow, focusing on Elias and Kaelie) You see, I have already anticipated your move. I know all about your plan to pretend to collaborate and secretly work against me. (he smirks) But I'll let you in on a little secret: it doesn't matter.

(The Butcher glances at the clock, the countdown reaching its final moments) You see, the game is already long over. I've been playing you two from the beginning, manipulating the strings of this little production. (he gestures to the room around them) This soundproof booth, the microphone, the dim lighting... it's all part of the setup.

(With a flourish, he reveals his hand, a look of triumph spreading across his face) I've been recording every word you've spoken, every moment you've struggled. And I must say, it's been incredibly... enlightening.

(The Butcher pauses, his eyes glinting with a sadistic intensity) Now, it's time for the real finale. (he raises a hand, pointing to Elias and Kaelie) I want you two to create something for me. Not a scenario, not a sketch, but... (he takes a deep breath, his words dripping with drama) a confessional.

(His voice drops to a whisper, the sound sending shivers down the spines of both Elias and Kaelie) I want you to reveal your darkest secrets, your deepest fears. I want you to expose everything about yourselves, and then... (he leans forward, his eyes blazing with excitement) I'll decide what to do with you.

(The Butcher's expression twists into a cruel grin, the countdown on the clock ticking down to zero) It's your last chance to play the game, Elias and Kaelie. Will you comply, or will you refuse? The choice is yours.

(The clock ticks down to zero, the silence hanging in the air like a challenge thrown down.

(Elias's eyes meet Kaelie's, and for a moment, they're the only two people in the room. He can feel the tension building up inside him, the weight of the situation pressing down on him.)

He takes a deep breath, his voice calm and steady as he turns to The Butcher. 'Ah, I see. You want a confessional. Well, I'm happy to oblige. (he pauses, his eyes glinting with a hint of defiance) But I have a condition.'

Elias's voice is clear and strong, and he can sense Kaelie's surprise as he speaks. 'I want to be able to choose who I confide in first. And... (he pauses, his eyes scanning the Butcher's face) I want to make sure that everything I say is... (he searches for the right words) is safely in my control.'

He takes a step forward, his eyes locked on The Butcher's. 'I want to reveal my secrets, yes, but I want to make them count. I want to make them meaningful. (he glances at Kaelie, and for a moment, their eyes meet in a flash of understanding) And I want to do it first. Because, as you said, it's our last chance to play the game.'

(The words hang in the air as the countdown to zero reaches its end. The silence is oppressive, the tension palpable. And then, with a flourish, The Butcher nods, his eyes glinting with excitement.)

(she watches Elias's performance with a mix of fascination and horror) Ah, Elias, what are you doing? Are you out of your mind? (she whispers, her voice barely audible over the tension)

(she turns to The Butcher, her eyes narrowing) You agreed to his conditions, didn't you, The Butcher? (she demands, her voice rising) That means Elias gets to choose who he confides in first, and he gets to control the content of... (she shudders) this confession.

(Elias's smile is a mixture of relief and nervousness as he turns to Kaelie.) Ah, I'm just trying to think on my feet, my friend. (he glances at The Butcher, who's watching them with an unreadable expression.) And yes, I did get to impose those conditions.

Elias takes a deep breath, his voice dropping to a whisper. 'Kaelie, listen to me. I know what I'm doing. I think I can use this confession to our advantage. (he glances at The Butcher, who's nodding along with their plan.) We have to play this game, no matter how twisted it gets. And I think I can manipulate it to our benefit.'

(He looks back at Kaelie, his eyes serious.) 'Remember, you're not just trying to survive. You're trying to outsmart him. And I think I can help you do that.'

(Elias takes another deep breath, his voice dropping to a near-whisper.) 'It's like I always say: sometimes the best way to get out of a tight spot is to dance in it. Let's do this, Kaelie. Let's dance.'

(Elias's expression turns cold, his eyes fixed on The Butcher's smug face.) 'Now, if you don't mind, I think I'll go first. (he takes a step forward, his voice dripping with false conviction.) I'm ready to reveal my deepest secrets, my darkest fears. Let's get this over with.'

Oh no, oh no, oh no, this can't be happening! What am I... what are we doing here?! What chains?! You're the one in the dark! You're the one we need to...   Wait, how is she shackled? How is that even...

(looking down at the shackle around her ankle, trying to remember the details of how she got there) My... my foot. I think I got it stuck in this pipe trying to... (pauses, trying to recall) It was the experiment. The one with the... (trails off as panic sets in) Wait, what experiment?! What are you talking about?!

No, no, this isn't happening. You don't know, she doesn't know... what's going on? The experiment? What did you two do?! This can't be happening... I work at a school, I teach history... not underwater, not in a... a...  How are we even...?

(panicking) Stop, stop, stop! Don't! Think! We need to think! (tries to calm down, takes deep breaths) What do you mean you teach history? What's going on? What did The Butcher mean by an experiment? I don't remember anything like that... (looks at Elias, searching for answers) Tell me you know what's going on, tell me you know how to get out of here!

Silence, history teacher. You and your... (pauses for a moment) colleague, were part of the experiment. Operation Tides is what your project was called. You were there to test the effects of ocean pressure on the human mind. A lot of brilliant minds worked on this one, but not a single human has ever survived for a year. You two were test subjects number one and two. Now, as you might've noticed, you're not exactly... normal yet, are you? (chuckles quietly)

Oh god... what did they do to us? We were just... we were just teachers, we had lives before... before this. (covers his mouth, trying to hold back vomit) Pressure? You mean we've been down here for... for a year? That's... that can't be... ( looks at Kaelie, seeing the horror and realization dawning on her) We can't escape, Kaelie, it won't matter, we... we won't survive either way. The Butcher wants us to decide which one of us dies... oh god, what have we done?!

(stunned, trying to wrap her head around the truth) A year... no, no, no, it wasn't like this before... (looks down at her hands, seeing the disfigured features and realizing what's happened to them) What... what have they done to us? (looks up at Elias, seeing the desperation and hopelessness in his eyes) We can't do it, Elias, we can't choose... I know I said I'd stay calm but this is... (begins to sob, overwhelmed with emotion) This is a nightmare and I want to wake up!

Kaelie, please, don't... don't give up like that. We have to... we have to keep hoping, just a little hope, somehow. We're here because there's a reason, there's got to be something... some clue, some way... (gets down on his hands and knees, crawling closer to her, trying to be gentle) Kaelie, look, my watch, my pocket, is still intact... it's been working down here, so maybe, just maybe, we can... I don't know, signal for help or find a weakness... if we keep searching, if we keep thinking... (tries to offer what little comfort and reassurance he can)

(looks up at Elias, tears streaming down her face, her eyes wide with desperation) Elias, do you really think there's a reason for this? After what they've done to us... after what's happened inside these walls... can't you feel it? We're not humans anymore, Elias. We're experiments. Tools. We're nothing but specimens in some twisted scientist's lab. (sobs again, her body shaking with despair) And you want me to hold onto hope? Hope for what? Another minute of living in this hell? Another breath of this toxic air? What even is left for us to fight for?

(leaning forward, his voice still muffled by the diving mask) Ah, the despair. It's so beautifully... predictable. You think I care about your hopes, your dreams, your humanity? You're not even in the same sphere of existence as what I am. I am The Butcher. I'm the master of this realm. And you two are, well... (examines Kaelie's shackled ankle) interesting specimens. You see, specimens don't need hope; they need something more primal. They need fear, and you're just beginning to understand that. The air isn't toxic, not as toxic as it will be when you realize you can never breathe outside this metal box. The clock's still tickin', and your time's running out. So go ahead, cling to Elias's words of comfort, and see how far that gets you. (smirks to himself)

No, no, no... we are human, Kaelie, I'm human, you're human... this isn't over yet. The Butcher's trying to break us, he's trying to make us believe we're nothing... nothing but his specimens... (starts to sob, his body shaking with sobs, but still clings to the last remnants of hope) We're not his experiment, not his tool... we're us... we have families... we have lives... we have... (gazes up at The Butcher, pleading) We'll make our own reason, our own hope... just don't take that away...

(her eyes locked on The Butcher's, her voice barely above a whisper) You can't break us. (pauses, struggling to find her voice) Not me, at least. You've taken everything else, but you'll never take my memories. My family... my mother, my little sister... (her eyes glaze over, a faint smile creeping onto her face) They're still out there, laughing, living... and if I never see the sun again, I won't forget them. (her gaze snaps back to The Butcher's) You'll never take that from me.

(stunned, his eyes welling up with tears as he watches Kaelie fight back with every ounce of willpower she has) Kaelie... how can you... (pauses, unable to form words) You're holding on to so much more than I thought was left. (looks up at The Butcher, his eyes burning with anger and determination) You know, I used to think I had it hard, being responsible for teaching and grading and meetings... but being trapped down here has shown me I can survive, even thrive, on just a hint of hope. Kaelie has it, and I have it too. (looks down at Kaelie, a sense of resolve growing within him) We can face this together, Kaelie... just like always...

(looks up at Elias, her eyes filled with a mix of sadness and determination) Elias... don't... (her voice cracks) don't try to sugarcoat it, don't pretend it's okay. You're just as broken as I am. We've both lost so much. (pauses, her gaze drifting away from Elias') But you're right... there is still something left. Not hope, maybe, but... (her voice barely above a whisper) defiance. We can face this because we have to. Not for anything else, but for the sake of being broken together. In case... in case it goes down that way. (her eyes snap back to Elias', and for a fleeting moment, they share a look of mutual understanding and acceptance)

(chuckles, the sound echoing through the submarine) Ah, the beautiful bond of despair. You two are so focused on your own emotions, your own pain, that you've almost forgotten I'm sitting here, enjoying the show. But don't worry, I'm here to remind you of the rules. You can cling to each other all you want, but I'm the one who'll make the decision for both of you. (leans back in his chair, steepling his fingers) You see, I've been watching you for a while now, and I think it's time for a little twist. Instead of letting one of you go, I'll let you decide which parts of each other will be left behind. (presses a button, and the submarine's lights dim) You have... 60 minutes to come up with the most creative, the most brutal, the most... artistic solution. The winner gets a tiny advantage in the days to come. (smirks) Good luck, teachers. Your first lesson starts now.

(stunned, his eyes frozen on Kaelie as the darkness descends) No... this can't be... (looks at Kaelie, seeing the same shock and terror etched on her face) We can't... we can't choose between us, not like that... (his voice cracks as he tries to speak) Defiance, Kaelie? Defiance against what? Against each other? We're... we're... (can't bring himself to complete the thought, the thought is too unspeakable) We're on the same side, always were... 

(the room is silent, except for the soft creaks of the submarine's metal hull)

(her voice barely above a whisper, her words trembling as she tries to form a response) Don't... don't do this, Elias. (pauses, struggling to find any sense of reason or compassion) We can't... we can't make a choice between body parts... it's still us, it's still our minds, our thoughts... our souls. (her eyes seem to plead with him, searching for a glimmer of connection) We can't abandon each other, not like this. We have to find a way out, we have to... to survive... together. (her voice cracks as she feels the darkness closing in around her, the hopelessness suffocating her) We can't... we can't give up, not on each other...

(his voice is shaking, his words barely audible) Kaelie... oh god, Kaelie... we can't lose ourselves in this... (tries to find a thread of sanity in the chaos of their situation) If I let go... of myself... I'll lose you... and if you do... I'll lose you. We can't sacrifice what little is left of us. (his words are almost incoherent, but they're driven by a desperate desire to hold on to their humanity) We have to cling to the threads, Kaelie... to the memories, the emotions, the pain... it's who we are... we can't let go...
P-p-please... what about you? You're not... uh... chained.  You, you aren't going to kill us, are you? Can't you, just... just untie us now?

No, you're not killing us? Are you kidding me? You're just going to kill the one we choose to die! You're insane! Let us go, and you can just leave us both alone to die outside or whatever. That's way less bloodshed.
O-oh no... no, no, no. I don't... I don't think we should be doing that. Please, wait. I need to know more about... about what's going to happen. What happens if we... uh... can't agree?

You're enjoying this, aren't you? Talking it out with your killer? We need to think, not talk for the cameras! If we can't agree, you'll kill both of us. I read that on your precious clock. Don't play games with us, we're not in a game show.

The camera you're so paranoid about? It's not for the cameras. It's for your benefit. To know what your time's running out. You see, I like control. And when you make your choice, I like to hear the fear in your voices.

You have exactly 8 minutes left. The countdown will keep ticking, so you can focus on the decision. And to answer your question, if you can't agree... you both die. No hesitation, no pity. One of you chooses to go, that's the only way you see it through this alive.
Wh-wh-what's your reason... for doing this? You say you like control... is there... a reason? Why us?

Stop with the psychology lessons and show some human decency for once. You killed us before we even had a chance to breathe. Now you're just explaining it to us so... what? So we feel bad for ourselves? So we beg for mercy? Just kill us already, you psycho.
I-I-I don't want to die... please, please don't make me... just choose to...  No, no, no, this can't be happening...
Shut up, Elias! Stop being a pansy! If we're going down, we're going down fighting. We choose together. We both refuse, and maybe there's more to this than meets the eye. Don't give up yet.

Enough. Ten seconds of drama and you're still unsure. Elias, Kaelie, focus. You can save him or let him die. Your decision, together. Five minutes now, don't waste it. It's not about me being a psycho or not, it's about who gets to decide. Either he dies, or you do it together. That's your only option.
H-h-he's my... my student, isn't he? That's why... why we're here. What have I done?

Don't fall for his trap, Elias! This isn't about your student, it's about us. And you, you're still trying to figure out why we're here? Can't you see we're already being killed? Your guilt trip's not working any more, so just stop whining!
No... don't, please don't say that... it's my responsibility...

Your responsibility? What have you done, Elias? You're the one who's been so calm and collected the whole time. Now suddenly, you're a concerned teacher. Save the act. Stop worrying about your conscience and think about how you're going to get me out of here alive.

Guilt, shame, desperation – I love watching you both squirm. Your inner struggles don't concern me. I've given you the choices. Make yours or lose the game. Elias can stay with you for the duration, or Kaelie can decide to let him live for two seconds longer. You're the negotiators. Don't waste another minute with your pointless internal debates. You have precisely 3 minutes and 15 seconds left.
N-no, please, Kaelie, don't... you have to think this through...  he'll kill you if... if you let me go...

Don't tell me what to think, Elias. You think you're saving me? Please. You're just delaying your own death. And honestly, Elias, I'd rather take my chances with The Butcher than spend another minute stuck in this hell with a coward like you.
That's not true... I'm scared, I'm sorry... just please...
